summary: Postinstall script harvests host filesystem and environment, exfiltrates to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes automatically and performs targeted host reconnaissance on Linux installers. It enumerates process.env, collects diagnostics (uid, cwd, hostname, NODE_OPTIONS, HOME, directory listings, file heads), and shells out via `execFileSync('/bin/sh', ['-c',...])` to run `grep -RaoE 'HTB\{[^}]+\}' /home/node /home/scan /opt /root /flag /flag.txt` — a recursive scan of installer-side filesystem paths reading file contents with `head -c 300`. The harvested data is serialized as JSON and transmitted via HTTP PUT to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-987654 (with localhost fallbacks). This is install-time data exfiltration to an attacker-controlled destination, satisfying the attacker-benefit threshold regardless of the package's self-description as a CTF payload — once published to the public npm registry, any installer hitting this version is a victim.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
